Name: La Pepica
Tips: Valencia's most famous paella restaurant since 1898. Hemingway ate here. Paella lunch only 13:00-16:00. On the beachfront. Reference paella valenciana with rabbit and chicken.

Name: Casa Roberto
Tips: Excellent paella valenciana near Old Town. One of most recommended by locals. Lunch only for paella. Book ahead.

Name: Canalla Bistro
Tips: Ricard Camarena's casual restaurant in Ruzafa. Creative tapas global influences fun atmosphere. Book ahead. Good dinner option Aug 27.

Name: Horchatería Santa Catalina
Tips: THE horchata institution of Valencia since 1936. Horchata de chufa + fartons. Breakfast option morning of Aug 28. Open from 08:00.

Name: El Rinconet
Tips: Small local restaurant in Ruzafa loved by locals. Good dinner service with traditional rice dishes. Walk-in usually fine.

Name: La Fusta
Tips: Traditional Valencian food local crowd. Reliable walk-in dinner option.

Name: El Poblet
Tips: Ricard Camarena's Michelin-starred restaurant. Book weeks ahead. Special occasion splurge.

Name: Mercado de Colón
Tips: Beautiful 1916 Modernist building. Good drinks and light dinner option. More refined atmosphere.

Name: Riff
Tips: One of Valencia's best modern restaurants. Bernd Knöller's seasonal tasting menu. A splurge option. Book well ahead.

Name: Mercado Central
Address: Plaça de la Ciutat de Bruges Valencia
Tips: One of the great market buildings of Europe — 1928 Modernista dome of iron, tile, and stained glass. Excellent for buying train snacks from charcutería/cheese stalls. Opens 07:30.
